mechanism_narrative: Extremely low federal funds rates, sustained over several months, indicate an accommodative monetary policy stance aimed at stimulating economic activity. This reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, leading to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate from elevated levels signals a strengthening labor market and improving consumer confidence. This translates into increased consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops by >0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable or declining inflation indicates robust consumer demand without overheating. This environment supports corporate revenue growth and profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth < 2%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are negative or flat when both conditions are met, or if the correlation between the combined signal and returns is near zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates a healthy manufacturing sector and expanding supply capacity. This typically correlates with strong economic growth and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns, especially during periods of economic expansion.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth above 0.5% over 3 months consistently leads to negative S&P 500 20-day returns during periods identified as economic expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts indicates growing confidence in the economy and future demand, stimulating construction and related industries. This positive sentiment and economic activity translate into higher equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 10% month-over-month
failure_condition: If HOUST increases by >10% MoM but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a robust job market, leading to increased consumer income and spending. This boosts corporate revenues and earnings, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth above 0.1% over 3 months consistently leads to negative S&P 500 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Unexpectedly high and accelerating inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, while also increasing the likelihood of monetary tightening. This combination typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns as discount rates rise and earnings expectations fall.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 4% consistently coincides with positive S&P 500 5-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which generally translates to higher corporate profits and investor confidence. This fundamental strength drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth above 2.5% QoQ annualized consistently leads to negative S&P 500 60-day returns.
